description_long: This object consists of a group of five rustic wooden crates arranged in a staggered, stacked formation. Each crate is constructed from weathered wooden planks, featuring prominent diagonal and horizontal bracing that forms an 'X' pattern on each visible face. The wood appears aged, with visible grain, knots, and areas of discoloration, suggesting prolonged outdoor use or storage. The crates vary slightly in size, with some stacked atop others and one crate partially open, its lid propped up, revealing a hollow interior. The overall structure is boxy and modular, with gaps between some crates, and the open crate hints at storage or containment functionality. This arrangement is typical of storage areas, garages, or urban back alleys where goods or materials are temporarily kept.
description: A group of five weathered wooden crates, some stacked and one partially open, showing rustic textures and visible wood grain.
description_view_0: Front view showing five crates, with one crate partially open on the right and the 'X' bracing pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical stacking of crates and the open crate's lid extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the crates, with the open crate's lid more visible and the staggered arrangement apparent.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the two largest crates stacked vertically, with the open crate less visible.
